methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $400M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $310M

data.narrative:
Pano AI is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Sonia Kastner, currently valued at $500M+ post-money on $89M total raised. The relevant comp universe consists of one public company (LNZA) and eight private peers in the same sector and stage, with four directly comparable Series B companies: Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M).

Two valuation methods apply here. The stage-progression approach, anchoring to the $500M last post-money valuation and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $950M. The peer-median method draws from the four same-stage, same-sector comps, which span $120M to $400M with a median of $305M, reflecting broader market pricing for the sector.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $259M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $628M. The current $500M+ valuation sits within this range but toward the lower-middle portion, suggesting modest upside room if execution justifies stage progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peer valuations (3.3x range from min to max) and whether Pano AI's specific technology and market position warrant positioning above or below the $305M peer median. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median methods ($950M vs. $305M) underscores sensitivity to methodology choice.
